tasks.title: Create Alembic migrations for calls and call_configs tables
tasks.description: Create new database tables required by the voice service using Alembic migrations against the existing PostgreSQL database.
tasks.details:
Create two new tables:

1. **calls** table:
- id (uuid, primary key)
- lead_id (bigint, FK to leads)
- law_firm_id (bigint, FK to law_firms)
- campaign_id (bigint, FK to campaigns, nullable)
- vapi_call_id (string, Vapi's call identifier)
- status (string enum: initiated, ringing, in_progress, completed, failed, no_answer, voicemail)
- call_type (string enum: initial_outreach, follow_up, appointment)
- prompt_used (text)
- transcript (text)
- recording_url (string)
- duration_seconds (integer)
- outcome (string enum: interested, not_interested, voicemail_left, no_answer, callback_requested, wrong_number, do_not_call)
- extracted_data (jsonb)
- error_message (text)
- initiated_at, connected_at, completed_at, created_at, updated_at (timestamps)

2. **call_configs** table:
- id (uuid, primary key)
- law_firm_id (bigint, FK to law_firms)
- call_type (string enum)
- system_prompt_template (text)
- voice_id (string)
- llm_model (string)
- max_duration_seconds (integer, default 300)
- retry_attempts (integer, default 2)
- retry_delay_minutes (integer, default 60)
- active (boolean)
- created_at, updated_at (timestamps)

Add appropriate indexes on lead_id, law_firm_id, vapi_call_id, status, and created_at. Use additive migrations only — do not modify existing tables.
tasks.testStrategy: Run alembic upgrade head against a test database. Verify tables exist with correct columns, types, constraints, and indexes. Run alembic downgrade to verify reversibility.

tasks.title: Implement Pydantic schemas for all request/response models
tasks.description: Create Pydantic v2 schemas for API request/response validation covering call initiation, webhooks, and call status responses.
tasks.details:
Create schemas in api/app/schemas/:

**calls.py:**
- CallInitiateRequest: lead_id, law_firm_id, campaign_id (optional), trigger_event, lead_details (name, phone, case_type, source, etc.)
- CallInitiateResponse: call_id, status
- CallResponse: full call record (id, lead_id, status, transcript, outcome, extracted_data, timestamps, etc.)
- CallListResponse: list of CallResponse
- CallRetryResponse: new_call_id, status

**webhooks.py:**
- VapiWebhookPayload: vapi_call_id, type (call.started, call.ended, call.transcript), transcript, recording_url, duration, status, ended_reason, etc.
- VapiCallEndedPayload: extends base with transcript, summary, recording

All schemas should use Pydantic v2 syntax (model_config, field_validator, etc.).
tasks.testStrategy: Unit test each schema with valid and invalid payloads. Test serialization/deserialization round-trips. Test validation error messages.

tasks.title: Implement API key authentication middleware
tasks.description: Add API key authentication for Rails-to-FastAPI communication and a dependency injection pattern for protected endpoints.
tasks.details:
Create api/app/core/auth.py:

1. **API Key validation dependency:**
- Read API_KEY from settings
- Check X-API-Key header on incoming requests
- Return 401 if missing or invalid
- Use FastAPI Depends() pattern

2. **Vapi webhook signature verification:**
- Verify Vapi webhook signatures using VAPI_WEBHOOK_SECRET
- Separate dependency for webhook endpoints

3. **Update config.py:**
- Add API_KEY, VAPI_API_KEY, VAPI_WEBHOOK_SECRET, ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, RAILS_API_URL, RAILS_API_KEY to Settings

4. **Update .env.example** with all new env vars.
tasks.testStrategy: Test valid API key passes, invalid key returns 401, missing key returns 401. Test Vapi signature verification with known good/bad signatures.

tasks.title: Implement Vapi client service
tasks.description: Create an async HTTP client wrapping Vapi's REST API for placing outbound calls and retrieving call details.
tasks.details:
Create api/app/services/calling.py:

1. **VapiClient class:**
- __init__ with api_key, base_url (https://api.vapi.ai)
- Uses httpx.AsyncClient with connection pooling
- Proper timeout configuration (30s connect, 60s read)

2. **Methods:**
- create_call(phone_number: str, assistant_config: dict) -> VapiCallResponse
  - POST to /call/phone with assistant override, phone number, etc.
- get_call(vapi_call_id: str) -> VapiCallResponse
  - GET /call/{id}

3. **Error handling:**
- Retry on 429 (rate limit) with exponential backoff
- Raise specific exceptions: VapiAPIError, VapiRateLimitError, VapiCallFailedError
- Log all API calls with request/response details

4. **Dependency injection:**
- Create get_vapi_client() dependency that returns configured client
tasks.testStrategy: Unit test with httpx mock. Test successful call creation, rate limit retry, error handling. Integration test with Vapi sandbox if available.

tasks.title: Implement chatbot prompt builder service
tasks.description: Create a service that builds dynamic, personalized conversation prompts from lead details, law firm config, and call templates.
tasks.details:
Create api/app/services/chatbot.py:

1. **PromptBuilder class:**
- build_prompt(lead: dict, law_firm: LawFirms, call_config: CallConfigs | None) -> dict
  - Returns Vapi assistant config: { model, voice, system_prompt, first_message }

2. **Default template** (used when no call_config exists):
- System prompt includes: firm name, lead name, case type, reason for call
- Professional, empathetic tone appropriate for legal services
- Key objectives: verify interest, qualify the lead, schedule callback/consultation
- Guardrails: don't provide legal advice, don't make promises, handle objections gracefully

3. **Template rendering:**
- Support Jinja2-style templates from call_configs.system_prompt_template
- Template variables: lead_name, lead_phone, case_type, firm_name, firm_phone, source, etc.

4. **Voice and model config:**
- Default voice selection (professional, clear)
- Model selection from call_config or default to claude-sonnet-4-6
tasks.testStrategy: Unit test prompt generation with various lead/firm combinations. Verify all template variables are substituted. Test default template and custom templates. Test edge cases (missing fields, empty values).

tasks.title: Implement call initiation endpoint
tasks.description: Create the POST /api/v1/calls/initiate endpoint that receives requests from Rails, builds the chatbot, and places the call via Vapi.
tasks.details:
Create api/app/routers/calls.py:

1. **POST /api/v1/calls/initiate:**
- Protected by API key auth dependency
- Validates request body with CallInitiateRequest schema
- Idempotency check: query calls table for active call to same lead_id within configurable window (default 24h)
- Fetch law_firm and lead details from DB
- Fetch call_config for the law firm + call type (or use defaults)
- Build chatbot prompt via PromptBuilder
- Create call record in DB with status 'initiated'
- Call Vapi API to place the call
- Update call record with vapi_call_id
- Return CallInitiateResponse with call_id and status

2. **Error handling:**
- If Vapi call fails, update call record to 'failed' with error_message
- Return appropriate HTTP error codes (409 for duplicate, 502 for Vapi failure)

3. **Register router** in app/main.py with prefix /api/v1
tasks.testStrategy: Test successful call initiation end-to-end with mocked Vapi. Test idempotency rejection. Test invalid lead_id. Test Vapi failure handling. Test auth required.

tasks.title: Implement Vapi webhook endpoint
tasks.description: Create the POST /api/v1/calls/webhook endpoint to receive and process post-call data from Vapi.
tasks.details:
Add to api/app/routers/calls.py:

1. **POST /api/v1/calls/webhook:**
- Verify Vapi webhook signature
- Parse webhook payload (different event types: call.started, call.ended, call.transcript)
- Handle each event type:
  - call.started -> update call status to 'in_progress', set connected_at
  - call.ended -> update status to 'completed' or 'failed', store transcript, recording_url, duration
  - status-update -> update intermediate statuses (ringing, etc.)
- For call.ended events, dispatch background task for transcript analysis
- Return 200 OK immediately (processing is async)

2. **Idempotent processing:**
- Use vapi_call_id to look up call record
- Skip if already processed (e.g. completed call getting duplicate webhook)

3. **Error handling:**
- Log but don't fail on unknown event types
- Return 200 even on processing errors (so Vapi doesn't retry endlessly)
tasks.testStrategy: Test each webhook event type with sample Vapi payloads. Test signature verification. Test idempotent processing of duplicate webhooks. Test unknown event type handling.

tasks.title: Implement transcript analysis service with Claude API
tasks.description: Create a service that uses Claude API to parse call transcripts and extract structured data (outcome, sentiment, key information).
tasks.details:
Create api/app/services/post_call.py:

1. **TranscriptAnalyzer class:**
- Uses anthropic Python SDK with async client
- analyze(transcript: str, lead_context: dict) -> ExtractedCallData

2. **Extraction prompt:**
- Classify call outcome: interested, not_interested, voicemail_left, no_answer, callback_requested, wrong_number, do_not_call
- Rate lead interest level (1-5)
- Extract key information mentioned by lead (preferred callback time, questions, concerns, case details)
- Determine recommended next action
- Return as structured JSON

3. **ExtractedCallData schema:**
- outcome (enum)
- interest_level (int 1-5)
- key_information (dict)
- recommended_action (string)
- summary (string, 2-3 sentence summary)

4. **process_completed_call(call_id: uuid):**
- Fetch call record with transcript
- Run transcript analysis
- Update calls table: outcome, extracted_data
- Update leads table with relevant info
- Send callback to Rails API with results
tasks.testStrategy: Test with sample transcripts covering each outcome type. Verify structured extraction accuracy. Test with empty/short transcripts. Test Claude API error handling.

tasks.title: Implement Rails callback notification service
tasks.description: Create a service to notify the Rails API when calls complete, sending back structured results.
tasks.details:
Create api/app/services/rails_callback.py:

1. **RailsClient class:**
- Uses httpx.AsyncClient
- Authenticates with RAILS_API_KEY
- Base URL from RAILS_API_URL setting

2. **Methods:**
- notify_call_completed(call_id, lead_id, outcome, extracted_data) -> bool
  - POST to Rails callback endpoint with call results
  - Retry up to 3 times on failure
  - Log success/failure

3. **Integration:**
- Called from post_call.process_completed_call() after transcript analysis
- Fire-and-forget pattern — log errors but don't fail the webhook processing
tasks.testStrategy: Test successful callback with mocked Rails endpoint. Test retry logic on failures. Test timeout handling.

tasks.title: Implement call management read endpoints
tasks.description: Create GET endpoints for querying call status and history.
tasks.details:
Add to api/app/routers/calls.py:

1. **GET /api/v1/calls/{call_id}:**
- Protected by API key auth
- Return full call record with CallResponse schema
- 404 if not found

2. **GET /api/v1/calls/lead/{lead_id}:**
- Protected by API key auth
- Return list of calls for the lead, ordered by created_at desc
- Optional query params: status filter, limit, offset

3. **POST /api/v1/calls/{call_id}/retry:**
- Protected by API key auth
- Only retry failed calls
- Create new call record linked to same lead
- Return new call_id
tasks.testStrategy: Test get by call_id (found and not found). Test get by lead_id with multiple calls. Test retry on failed call. Test retry rejection on non-failed call.

tasks.title: Add structured logging and error handling
tasks.description: Implement structured JSON logging and global error handling for production observability.
tasks.details:
1. **Structured logging:**
- Configure Python logging with JSON formatter
- Log all API requests with request_id, method, path, status, duration
- Log all Vapi API calls with request/response
- Log all DB operations for debugging
- Include call_id and lead_id in log context where available

2. **Global error handling:**
- Add FastAPI exception handlers for common errors
- Return consistent error response format: { error: string, detail: string, request_id: string }
- Don't leak internal errors to clients

3. **Request ID middleware:**
- Generate UUID request_id for each request
- Include in all log entries and error responses
- Return in X-Request-ID response header
tasks.testStrategy: Verify log output format is valid JSON. Test error responses have consistent format. Test request_id propagation through request lifecycle.

tasks.title: Production Dockerfile and Cloud Run deployment config
tasks.description: Optimize the Dockerfile for production and add Cloud Build configuration for GCP deployment.
tasks.details:
1. **Optimize Dockerfile:**
- Multi-stage build for smaller image
- Non-root user
- Health check endpoint
- Proper signal handling for graceful shutdown

2. **cloudbuild.yaml:**
- Build Docker image
- Push to Artifact Registry
- Deploy to Cloud Run
- Set environment variables from Secret Manager

3. **Cloud Run config:**
- Min instances: 0, Max instances: 10
- Memory: 512Mi, CPU: 1
- Concurrency: 80
- Port: 8080
- Secrets: DATABASE_URL, VAPI_API_KEY, VAPI_WEBHOOK_SECRET, ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, API_KEY, RAILS_API_URL, RAILS_API_KEY

4. Update .env.example with all required variables.
tasks.testStrategy: Build Docker image locally and verify it starts. Test health endpoint in container. Verify secrets are not baked into image.
